records.ety: [AS. singan; akin to D. zingen, OS. & OHG. singan, G. singen, Icel. syngja, Sw. sjunga, Dan. synge, Goth. siggwan, and perhaps to E. say, v.t., or cf. Gr. <?/<?/<?/ voice. Cf. Singe, Song.]
records.morph: [imp. Sung (?) or Sang (<?/); p. p. Sung; p. pr. & vb. n. Singing.]

defs: To utter sounds with musical inflections or melodious modulations of voice, as fancy may dictate, or according to the notes of a song or tune, or of a given part (as alto, tenor, etc.) in a chorus or concerted piece.
quotes: The noise of them that sing do I hear.

defs: To utter sweet melodious sounds, as birds do.
quotes: On every bough the briddes heard I sing.
Singing birds, in silver cages hung.

defs: To make a small, shrill sound; as, the air sings in passing through a crevice.
quotes: O'er his head the flying spear Sang innocent, and spent its force in air.

defs: To tell or relate something in numbers or verse; to celebrate something in poetry.
quotes: Bid her . . . sing Of human hope by cross event destroyed.
They should sing if thet they were bent.

defs: To utter with musical inflections or modulations of voice.
quotes: And they sing the song of Moses, the servant of God, and the song of the Lamb.
And in the darkness sing your carol of high praise.

defs: To celebrate in song; to give praises to in verse; to relate or rehearse in numbers, verse, or poetry.
quotes: Arms and the man I sing.
The last, the happiest British king, Whom thou shalt paint or I shall sing.

defs: To influence by singing; to lull by singing; as, to sing a child to sleep.
To accompany, or attend on, with singing.
quotes: I heard them singing home the bride.
